name: Segmentation
description: Divide an object or system into independent parts.
guidance: Split large components into modules
Separate workflows into isolated stages
software_examples: Break a monolith into bounded modules

name: Taking out
description: Separate an interfering part or property from an object, or isolate only the necessary part.
guidance: Isolate heavy responsibilities from the hot path
software_examples: Move indexing or analytics out of request handling

name: Local quality
description: Transition from homogeneous structure to differentiated parts optimized for local conditions.
guidance: Tailor subsystems to their workloads
software_examples: Use different storage strategies per data domain

name: Asymmetry
description: Replace a symmetrical form with an asymmetrical one when it improves performance.
guidance: Use different read and write strategies
software_examples: Optimize read-heavy endpoints differently from write paths

name: Merging
description: Bring identical or similar objects or operations together.
guidance: Consolidate repetitive services
software_examples: Combine redundant pipelines into one orchestrated job

name: Universality
description: Make a part or object perform multiple functions, eliminating the need for other parts.
guidance: Reuse existing infrastructure to avoid new services
software_examples: Use an existing message bus for orchestration events

name: Nested doll
description: Place one object inside another or arrange systems hierarchically.
guidance: Encapsulate subsystems inside clear higher-level modules
software_examples: Wrap provider-specific clients behind a single orchestration layer

name: Anti-weight
description: Compensate for weight or load using counterbalancing actions or resources.
guidance: Offset expensive operations with asynchronous processing
software_examples: Move expensive post-processing to a background queue

name: Preliminary anti-action
description: Prepare countermeasures in advance to reduce harmful effects.
guidance: Install safeguards before risky mutations
software_examples: Run validation and backup before applying a large diff

name: Preliminary action
description: Perform required changes in advance so the system is ready to act immediately.
guidance: Precompute or prefetch when the trigger is predictable
software_examples: Warm routing tables before serving traffic

name: Beforehand cushioning
description: Prepare emergency means to compensate for low reliability.
guidance: Add fallbacks and recovery paths
software_examples: Keep a safe rollback path for schema changes

name: Equipotentiality
description: Change working conditions to avoid raising or lowering an object in a field.
guidance: Reduce unnecessary movement between layers or environments
software_examples: Process data close to where it is stored

name: The other way round
description: Invert the action used to solve the problem.
guidance: Reverse control flow or ownership when it simplifies the design
software_examples: Pull data on demand instead of pushing it everywhere

name: Spheroidality
description: Replace linear parts or motion with curved or rotary ones where beneficial.
guidance: Use cyclic or event-driven flow instead of rigid linear flow
software_examples: Replace polling loops with event subscriptions

name: Dynamization
description: Allow characteristics of a system to change to be optimal at each stage of operation.
guidance: Prefer runtime-adjustable behavior over static policies
software_examples: Switch caching strategy based on load

name: Partial or excessive action
description: If exact action is difficult, use slightly less or slightly more.
guidance: Accept approximate intermediate states when exactness is expensive
software_examples: Use sampled metrics instead of full tracing everywhere

name: Another dimension
description: Move an object into two- or three-dimensional space or use additional dimensions.
guidance: Add a new abstraction axis instead of overloading one structure
software_examples: Split configuration by environment and capability

name: Mechanical vibration
description: Use oscillations or periodic processes.
guidance: Use periodic health checks or refresh cycles when continuous work is wasteful
software_examples: Run scheduled reconciliation instead of constant recomputation

name: Periodic action
description: Replace continuous action with periodic or pulsed action.
guidance: Batch expensive work into intervals
software_examples: Flush logs or metrics on intervals rather than every event

name: Continuity of useful action
description: Carry on work continuously; eliminate idle and intermittent actions.
guidance: Keep pipelines busy and remove needless waits
software_examples: Stream results incrementally instead of waiting for full completion

name: Skipping
description: Conduct a process or certain stages at high speed.
guidance: Skip or fast-track low-value stages
software_examples: Bypass deep analysis for trivial read-only requests

name: Blessing in disguise
description: Use harmful factors to achieve a positive effect.
guidance: Turn constraints into signals or optimizations
software_examples: Use backpressure to auto-throttle load spikes

name: Feedback
description: Introduce feedback to improve a process or action.
guidance: Feed runtime observations back into policy decisions
software_examples: Adjust retry strategy based on failure rates

name: Intermediary
description: Use an intermediary carrier process or object.
guidance: Insert an adapter layer to decouple systems
software_examples: Use an orchestrator service between UI and model providers

name: Self-service
description: Make an object serve itself by performing auxiliary helpful functions.
guidance: Let the system prepare or validate its own next step
software_examples: Generate local metadata from the repo instead of asking the user

name: Copying
description: Use a simple or inexpensive copy instead of a complex or fragile original.
guidance: Use snapshots, stubs, or mirrors where direct coupling is risky
software_examples: Analyze a lightweight summary instead of the whole repository

name: Cheap short-living objects
description: Replace an expensive durable object with multiple inexpensive disposables.
guidance: Prefer ephemeral artifacts over heavy persistent ones where safe
software_examples: Use temporary workers for burst processing

name: Mechanics substitution
description: Replace a mechanical means with a sensory, electric, or software-based one.
guidance: Replace manual coordination with software policy
software_examples: Use rule-based orchestration instead of manual review routing

name: Pneumatics and hydraulics
description: Use gas or liquid systems instead of solid parts where useful.
guidance: Prefer flexible flow-oriented mechanisms over rigid structures
software_examples: Use streaming pipelines instead of fixed batch blocks

name: Flexible shells and thin films
description: Use flexible membranes or thin films instead of solid structures.
guidance: Use thin abstraction layers instead of heavyweight frameworks
software_examples: Add a lightweight middleware instead of replacing the whole provider stack

name: Porous materials
description: Make an object porous or add porous elements.
guidance: Allow selective permeability between subsystems
software_examples: Expose only curated context to the analysis engine

name: Color changes
description: Change the color or transparency of an object to improve visibility or behavior.
guidance: Improve state visibility through UI indicators
software_examples: Show approval status badges in the editor

name: Homogeneity
description: Make interacting objects from the same material or properties.
guidance: Standardize contracts between modules
software_examples: Use one typed JSON schema across UI and server

name: Discarding and recovering
description: Discard consumed or unnecessary parts and recover them if needed.
guidance: Drop transient state when it no longer adds value
software_examples: Clear stale approval sessions when prompts materially change

name: Parameter changes
description: Change an object's physical state or operating parameters.
guidance: Tune policies dynamically through configuration
software_examples: Switch fail-open vs fail-closed by deployment mode

name: Phase transitions
description: Use phenomena occurring during phase transitions.
guidance: Exploit transition points between states for control
software_examples: Trigger heavier validation only when moving to apply stage

name: Thermal expansion
description: Use expansion or contraction effects.
guidance: Scale resources elastically based on load
software_examples: Increase analysis depth only for complex tasks

name: Strong oxidants
description: Use enriched or intensified environments.
guidance: Increase signal strength when weak cues are insufficient
software_examples: Escalate to richer context extraction for ambiguous prompts

name: Inert atmosphere
description: Replace a normal environment with a neutral one.
guidance: Use isolated environments to reduce side effects
software_examples: Run code transformations in a sandboxed review stage

name: Composite materials
description: Replace homogeneous materials with composites.
guidance: Combine complementary techniques into one solution
software_examples: Use heuristics plus typed rules plus LLM analysis together
